old_claim.note: Democracy & Republicanism is contextual infrastructure or shared knowledge, not a strict hard prerequisite.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained because the source-backed legal-system chronology predates democracy and republicanism by more than a millennium.

source_shape.source_locator: Schoyen Collection entry anchors the Ur-Nammu law code around 2100 BCE, long before Athenian democracy or Roman republican institutions.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Early written law-code systems existed in monarchic Sumerian contexts rather than democratic/republican civic systems.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Democracy and republicanism are later political forms, not prerequisites for written legal codes or early dispute-ordering institutions.

ontology_before: Early Legal Systems treated Democracy & Republicanism as a contextual prerequisite.
ontology_after: Early Legal Systems no longer depends on later civic constitutional forms.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: Early Legal Systems must not directly depend on Democracy & Republicanism unless narrowed to democratic/republican legal institutions.
would_reject_if: The node is re-scoped only to democratic/republican legal systems.
The graph splits legal systems and reserves this node for later civic legal institutions.
A source shows Ur-Nammu-style law codes should not count as legal-system evidence.
short_reason: Early written law codes predate democracy and republicanism.
